visualizerParams.title: Minimal Path Sum: Two Ways
visualizerParams.problemDescription: Fix the logic to correctly implement dynamic programming for the minimal path sum problem.
visualizerParams.buggyCode:
def solution(grid):
    rows = len(grid)
    cols = len(grid[0])
    # BUG: Deep copy issue or initialization error
    dp = [[0] * cols] * rows
    
    dp[0][0] = grid[0][0]
    for i in range(1, rows):
        for j in range(1, cols):
            # BUG: Recurrence only considers neighbors if they were already visited
            # BUG: Initializing first row/col is missing, leading to 0 sums
            dp[i][j] = grid[i][j] + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1])
            
    return dp[-1][-1]


if __name__ == "__main__":
    grid = [[1, 2], [3, 4]]
    print(solution(grid))
